items.briefing:
Targeted phishing attacks are a growing threat to Australian organisations, with new warnings highlighting how criminals use personalised "spear phishing" emails to trick staff into approving fraudulent payments. AI is making these scams harder to spot, allowing attackers to craft convincing messages at scale. Separately, a Chinese cybercriminal group known as TA4922 is expanding its attacks globally, which may include Australian targets.

On a positive note, Google is rolling out fake call detection on Android phones, which can help staff identify scam calls in real time. **Practical tip:** Before approving any payment request received by email, always verify it through a separate phone call to a known contact number — not one provided in the email itself.

featured.title: PhishByte warns spear phishing drives payment fraud losses
featured.quote: Spear phishing targeting Australian businesses is causing significant payment fraud losses.
